title: NASA's Amazing Moon Photos: Earthset and Solar Eclipse
summary: NASA has shared new pictures from the Artemis II astronauts who flew by the Moon. One photo shows an 'Earthset,' where Earth looks like it's setting behind the Moon's rocky surface. Another photo shows a solar eclipse, where the Moon blocks the Sun, making a bright ring called the corona. The astronauts took these photos during their trip back to Earth. They saw the eclipse for almost an hour because they were close to the Moon. These photos remind people of the famous Earthrise photo from 1968. NASA says the photos help us learn about space and see how beautiful our planet is from far away.

background_read: Space exploration involves sending people and machines to study planets, moons, and stars. NASA is the United States space agency that runs missions like Artemis to explore the Moon and beyond. Astronauts travel in spacecraft, take photos, and do experiments. Photos from space help us understand Earth and the universe better. The Artemis II mission is part of NASA's plan to send humans back to the Moon and eventually to Mars.
Article_Structure: WHO: NASA and the Artemis II astronauts
WHAT: Released photos of Earthset and a solar eclipse from the Moon fly-by
WHERE: Near the Moon during the Artemis II mission
WHY: To share new space discoveries and inspire people about space exploration

perspectives.description: The photos provide new data about the Moon and Earth, helping scientists learn more about space.
These images inspire people, especially kids, to be curious about space and protect Earth.
